2026-06-29.did: Scaffolded repo via Backstage (action typescript template). Resolved access chain — Backstage DL membership, Okta sync, GHEC team perms — via gheco. Fixed CI: corepack enable step, yarn.lock drift from Yarn 4.6.0 migration, missing globals ESLint dep. Merged CODEOWNERS pointing to @eg-internal/devex-release-engineering-team. Confirmed branch protection is an org-level ruleset, not repo settings. Added RE team as collaborator (Maintain) via gheco. Set up AI DLC framework files from awslabs/aidlc-workflows.
2026-06-29.contacts: Praveen — approved Backstage access. jdestefano — reviewed/approved Workday goals pre-internship.
2026-06-29.learned: Access provisioning at a large org involves multiple async steps chained together (DL membership, Okta sync cycles, then GHEC permission propagation) — front-loading access requests early avoids losing days later. Also learned branch protection can be enforced at an org level via rulesets rather than per-repo settings, which isn't obvious until you go looking for it in the wrong place.

2026-06-30.did: Drafted v0 input/output contract by reviewing RCP Action's action.yml (retry/timeout patterns, mode-style inputs, Splunk URL output conventions) and the Bruno v1-v2 collection (real Aerosol URL path structure, response fields).
2026-06-30.blocked: Exact header attachment format for aerosol_token is unresolved — confirmed as a Bruno secret variable, but not how it gets attached to requests.
2026-06-30.contacts: Joe — technical lead, reviewing contract draft.
2026-06-30.learned: Reading an existing reference implementation (RCP Action) before writing a new contract from scratch saves a lot of guesswork — the retry/timeout and output-naming conventions were already solved problems, just needed to be found and reused.
